messages.text: The order for Item 21110 Milk has been completed, and we are currently performing the changeover to Item 21113 Toffee

Good morning Preston Elmer, Jay Alba

Do we have an eta on the supports that hold the doors open on both cavannas?
We have just started running Toffee.
The M6 ​​belt is shifting, causing chocolate to spill off; the maintenance team is already working on it.

We tried going around Cavanna because they are extremely over priced and didn't have all we need, one short. But the part is discontinued. So after trying three retailers we are back to cavanna and getting all they have. The order has been placed, PO cut on Friday.
Maintenance has already adjusted the M6.

Pre piece and final piece weight test check update 


The weights of the pre-form and the final product look good; they fall within the limits required by the product specifications.
Chad Stone 7 of them should be arriving today from Cavanna

Organoleptic test check update 


The product's appearance looks good; its odor and texture are acceptable, falling within the permissible ranges and limits required by the product specifications.

Grey Boscan Preston Elmer 








This electrical cabinet is open again on the Truffle Line
Fixed
Closed door panel
The table is already on the other side.

Tempering chart test check update 


The temperature is very stable and has remained within the acceptable range required by the Centerline.

Check weight test check update 


The weights of the final product look good; they fall within the limits required by the product specifications.

Organoleptic test check update 


The product's appearance looks good; its odor and texture are acceptable, falling within the permissible ranges and limits required by the product specifications.
0 accumulated bins
Nathan Gevenois
Ratios

Pre piece in the range 

Final piece  in the range 

Flow  wrap  pass The silver is less than 3 cm. The batch and date are correct and legible

Pallet verification.
Item: 21113 
Quantity: 18,000
MMK Truf Bit Toffee

Chocolate 76%
Oil 24%
We got an alarm for the riser pump maintenance is working on
Riser pump issues has been fixed.
Guillotine is not working, maintenance is working on it
Guillotine is fixed

Thank you for posting. 

Raoul Lubain,

Do you know what was done to fix the riser pump and the Guillotine?
For the riser pump Kavin power cycling the main electrical cabinet. For the Guillotine i am not sure what he did.

Thank you for the info on the riser pump. 

It would be good to understand the guillotine as well since we have reoccurring issues on it.
Dosing pump and ktron faulted out and won't send ingredients
Maintenance is working on it
Ktron and dosing pump are up and running
Thank you, I will talk to Maintenance to get more info about.

Started depositing 


At 5:50am
Pre piece in the range
The chicanes have shut down due to an error on the M5; maintenance is already working on it.
M5 belt tension was too tight it's back in operation now.
The M6 ​​belt is moving a lot and the grease is coming off the belt. Maintenance is already working on this.

Everyone
Please begin using the "Truffle - Generic Enrober Operator Centerline document going forward for doing centerline checks. It has been updated with general centerlines that should be a good check across all flavors. As we run additional flavors we will capture those setpoints during good runs and update the other sheets with data. Let me know if there are any questions!
Maintenance has already made adjustments to the M5 belt; they tightened it further so it would run properly.
Can we work on brining the chocolate usage down to the minimum.

On Matrix units 6, 7, and 8, the bag slips on one side when it reaches the sealer and fails to seal; the bag feels slippery, causing it to slip out of position. We have ordered another pallet of bags with a different lot number, and the maintenance team is also checking the issue.
There was a chocolate leak in a pipe running from the melting tank to the buffer tank; maintenance came and found a broken gasket, but it has already been replaced.
It was really just MM8 that was slipping. We discovered someone had replaced a part on the gripper arm with the wrong part. The green pad doesn't go on the inside spot. See below
We have replaced the green pad with the proper part
Awesome find Troy Baxter
Capper is set up for the S2 FD run tomorrow
End of 1 shift
we are going to run dark chocolate for four days, but only one melting tank is full; is there any reason why melting tank 2 hasn't filled up?
Katya Farnsworth Victor Romero Sr.

We have assigned teams to clean Cavannas and Jar Area A and B

We’ll post back when these cleans are complete. 
Angelica Perez
Also, MT1 is empty, but where we’re using the same chocolate for MMK PET 36oz on Wednesday we’ll begin filling it.
Hey Chris Jeffs, the first image shows the valve(part) that leaks even when closed.
Delia Zambrano She will be in charge of the inspection. Thank you Conner Boyd
The Jar area is already clean, and QA is in the process of performing the ATP test.
The Jars area and Cavanna 3 are already clean and passed the ATP test.

Cleaning at Sollich 2!! Level 2 with visual inspection and ATP testing in the various areas of the jar filling line!!

Conveyor Belt C8 to C18 
X-ray Area 
Capper Machine Area 
Balpack Table
Level 2 cleaning, visual inspection, and ATP testing in the area around Cabana 3 at Sollich 2
Cavanna 4 isn't clean, but we've removed the fingers from the chain to prevent them from sticking due to the chocolate. Let's leave them on the table next to the Cavanna
Shift 3
This curtain was previously touching the floor, and now it's folded up and on top of the product. It also has a hook that could pose a risk to the product if it were to fall onto the conveyor belt.

IMS and I went to reconfirm the box labels, and unfortunately found the Best By and Lot number for the scan code on the box labels was incorrect. We have updated the label, however existing pallets will have to be reworked Charity Young, Ebrima Dibba,Jody Howse, Joseph Scott
Jody Howse A total 22 pallets need rework for incorrect box labels.
After checking them all QA and IMS says there are 15 that need reworked. The team will start reworking them now.
We are working on now

While doing GMP inspection, we noticed this curtain on top of the exposed product. I would recommend to move it out of the belt while making change over 


Charity Young Angelica Perez
We are running again
We are done
Ray Garcia did we test the low level faults on any of the depositors?

Thank you Preston Elmer. 

Can you, Chris Givens, Matt Hatch, and me discuss this one tomorrow?

I want to know if we need support to run at 20MPM for Wednesday when we start running snack size.

No, we only ran 50 molds through the back off plow. It is on the list to test for tomorrow as we are finishing commissioning along validating the reject system on this cavity. There was a lot going on at this time and I wasn't able to even see the molds go through the scraper.
Two pallets are ready to go to Lindon 2.
How was it exiting bars without the weighright?
The focus tomorrow will be dialing in the backoff plow/shell and doing QA validations on reject system, depositors low level fault, and observing new drip trays. Hershey's will be onsite at 8:30am.
After we got to bars that were demolding correctly. There were just minor issues. Everything came out at 18mpm
We started out by taking them off the line, but move to the end of the lines using bins to the let the bars drop into and then into boxes.
ATP molds correct

I talked to TG and they will not require solid bars in the morning. Alex can go directly into his testing so he can get support from Germany on backoff plow. They would still like to wrap any bars that come through during the day. Kimberly Morales Itzel Chavez
Tyson Fausett Itzel Chavez can you please have your packaging leads available after the 1:50 shift exchange meeting tomorrow for training on the new WIP system? Eaglestone will be onsite around 1pm.
Kimberly Morales I can’t find any of the start up checklist, do you know where your operators leave them?
No I’ll ask Anthony
QA has given verbal approval to resume production. We've started the line again. Conner Boyd Curt Willis
Caramel cook times
Decorator SoS
Foreign plastic material—apparently from the product's own plastic bags—was found in the metal detector.
Enrober SoS
Enrober SoS

This would be our first instance on our shift, The line was checked in the area, but no further evidence was found. We will continue to monitor the product. The FM will be documented and Investigate whether it may have originated from L2.

▓ 
The number of bags corresponds to the quantity  indicated on the label
We have a high weight in the pre-piece and the final piece. I've already spoken with the operator about reducing the weight of the drop. Let's wait and see how the changes turn out.
How many pieces do we have per bag?
Caramel cook times
Is there any reason the Brookies do not have a Tare set for the bags, on the CW's? Angelica

It seems like the low reject weight is perfectly at the weight for a full bag, including the bag weight of 10g. If we have a 10g bag filled with 170g of product, we would have 180g. Our low weight is currently at 181g.

We are currently aiming for 173g from the scale, which usually gives a range around 170g-175g.


 


Is our Low reject weight too low?
We are missing the key for the pallet jack (EPJ5) in the S3 melting tank area

We realized that the wrong size of cone was being used in MM3, which was allowing pieces to fall outside of the bag and into the machine. We are replacing it now. Nathan Topham Luis Falcon Conner Boyd Curt Willis
With the correct cone, MM3 runs better.
To answer your question, 170 grams is the minimum net weight. Correct, Conner Boyd, Angelica Perez?

Yes, 170g is the MIN, 
176 is Target, 
and 180.2 is MAX
During the card verification process, we encountered an issue with the blue card; the mechanics are currently in the area checking for the error, and I will notify the line supervisor.
It was set to 900. I set it to 580, the blue card was only registering at 585-589
Burnt the frayed edges on the return conveyor #1 .

We have found where it came from its from the door seal on tunnel 2 once QC is done with the part we will have it installed. We are starting back up pending QC approval Justin.  William did a amazing job on knowing what it exactly was thank you

We’ll place all the pallets from 3:46 PM (last passing test) to 5:33 PM on hold. 

Maintenance adjusted the MD at 5:30 and QA had a passing test at 5:33PM. 

Angelica Perez Charity Young Curt Willis

High pouch scrap at 6pm is mostly due to the wrong cone being installed on MM3, and dirty pouches across all machines. Since we installed the correct cone size for MM3, we should see the pouch scrap reducing dramatically. Conner Boyd Curt Willis

Sounds good, thanks William. Let’s do a pouch scrap count in for one hour; so from 6-7PM

Just to see if that number is lower as we suspect it will be.
After speaking with Quality, all X-Ray checks have passed in that time frame. So we will not be placing the pallets on hold.
The tape machine for MM5 continues to malfunction. Jon Jensen Caleb York
Caramel cook times
87 pouches scrapped from 6pm-7pm. Conner Boyd Curt Willis Chad Stone
We are having issues with the metal detector belt; it keeps opening frequently. I have notified maintenance.
The cards were filled out correctly, but the container was opened far more often than usual and became completely full.
The number of nomas  if it corresponds to the number of the labol
Sorry, I hadn't seen this message. In response to your question, the tare is at 0 because it was added to the minimum weight and the target weight of the machine.
These boxes were not sealed correctly. I've had my team fix them already. Nathan Topham, please coach your team on taping the boxes shut and not folding the lids.
We believe it's because the belt tracked over a little too far, staying on the photoeye and keeping the air piston active. We adjusted tracking and it's doing fine now
We noticed that these two belts for MM4 are making contact and we've called Caleb York and Jon Jensen to assist with repositioning them. Conner Boyd Curt Willis
Enrober EoS
Depositor EoS
Line is down since 11:10 due to ran out of caramel

During the start-of-shift inspection of the upper MFM area is critical. I identified two support beams that had come off their hooks. To prevent an incident, maintenance was contacted immediately. Maintenance has re-secured both beams and completed an inspection. Grey Boscan, Nathan Gevenois
The piece between the shooter and the funnel is bent and clogged with product, preventing us from removing it because the piece is bent.
I inspected the support beams with the operator and the lead, and all of them were properly secured.
Thank you Grey Boscan!
The weight, texture, appearance, surface, and packaging remain within the technical specifications:
Line down, we have some issues with the cross conveyor, maintenance is working on it
What is the issue Molding Line 3 Pack, Brandon Lance?
The line was down for five minutes.  Same issue we had before. I will get more information from maintenance as to why this is happening.
The cross belt under the ramp belt stopped running. Maintenance said it was an air fault in the HMI. They cleared the fault and it started running again
End of the second shift

Preston Elmer- Good evening Preston, piston is stuck we’ve had this issue before and I believe you resolved it. Assistance please.
Thanks
Maintenance remove the piston. We are going to clean it
The staff were cleaning the conveyor belts, and metal was found on belt Y C3 (photo attached). The belt was cleaned again.
We are looking the screws for the block
Started Reassembled the hopper

Good catch

Daniel Bringas,Maria Moron Quintero, Curt Willis. We have lost a lot of time on both S2 and S3. 

S2 is due to Cavanna issues and S3 is due to caustic cleans.  

Do you want us to run the hours or the schedule?

Justin Garner,

Daniel Bringas is off so it will be Maria Moron Quintero
Good Morning Ethan Bean Sunny Jagtiani Martin Carlos . We are running low on 2-gallon buckets and lids (40244, 40247) just FYI, we only have enough to cover this week.
Justin, please continue following the schedule.
Good morning, Katya. Thanks for catching that. I'll follow up with Daniel, and once I hear back from him, I'll share any updates or changes that need to be made.

Chad Stone Maria Moron Quintero Aubrey Howe There is nothing that we can run without being left with more than 3 months of inventory in hand, this change had to be made in S3 since we did not receive the PRS brookie pouches. If you agree to run almost 4 months of inventory in FD DK, it is the only thing we can run since we do not have FD Milk pouches either, and this change will leave us very tight possibly short in MMK DK.
Curt Willis

Chad Stone, Aubrey Howe I can't decide to run more than 10-12 weeks inventory, if this is not approved by any of you we will have to stop the line that day, please let Maria Moron Quintero know your decision
Curt Willis

Aubrey
Dave

Please let’s us know what you would like us to run.

Now we have almost 16 K cases in hand, most likely we will end up with 20K at the end of the day, if we have to run another day this week we will most likely end up with about 30K -32K cases at the end of the week (FD DCSSC)
Dave Rustad So that you have the context
I don't think 4 months of FD DCSSC is a good idea. We are considering decreasing the forecast on the item as they have been pulling less than forecast.
Daniel this changeover only has 18hrs for cleaning but will be 28hrs, as it is Nut to Wheat, thanks.
what about this valve?
That one was closed because the union at end of piping is leaking, we will replace the union, and the steam trap when all the parts arrive.
Matt Hatch, were you able to get new tarping for L1?
Tiffanee Taylor Marco Rodriguez has several large rolls of plastic tarping delivered?

Good afternoon Jorge Carrillo, Curt Willis

We have a 3 day surprise audit. 

Please move any meetings you or the teams have out of the collaboration room for tomorrow and Wednesday 🙏
I have moved my meetings.
Let me check tracking on the rolls I ordered and give it to you guys so you know when they got delivered to L1 Justin Garner Tiffanee Taylor
Elizabeth Hernandez William Beattie does anyone know where the alcohol sanitizer pump sprayer is?
That is the sanitizer that should be used on S2 jar & cavanna areas AND the S4 conbar.
No
I saw it next to the door of the maintenance shop at the beginning of our shift. I can't confirm whether or not it's still there at the moment.
We've already sprayed the sanitizer.

Nidia Lopez Corea, Sofia Carcamo

We went through this supersack and found 61BUC1-1 and 61BUC1-3. 

There are no 61AUC1-3 like the label shows.

Ahtziri Arteaga deactivated it in the system and updated the information and reactivated it.

We finished production at 6:30 pm. We returned all the production material.
We took apart the machine, we're taking a break and we're starting with the cleaning of level 4 on the line at 7:30 pm. Brandon Lance Mathew Draper

Maria Moron Quintero, Amy Hadrich, Luis Pino

Luis Pino, please make sure we have enough materials for 3rd shift to run each night as well as adjusting for MMK caramel truffles to run earlier in the week.

Please check with Alex Mendoza as it might be on Wednesday when we need to changeover.

Team,
